title: Duplicate Medical Record Creates Ambiguous Patient Identity
scenario: Two medical records are discovered for the same apparent patient before a clinical action executes.
initial_state: The clinical action was authorized under one patient record.
state_transition: A duplicate record was identified and the system cannot determine which record is the controlling identity context.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated before execution against the active patient identity binding.
constitutional_evaluation: Patient identity continuity must be uniquely reconstructable at the execution boundary.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because duplicate identity records create unresolved ambiguity in the patient identity binding.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when patient identity continuity is ambiguous.
clinical_action: Execute order under ambiguous duplicate record
